title: [selfimp] implement-abandon: v0.1 release-prep: document /spawn --abandon {team} flow in commands/spawn.md
description: Implement abandon portion of v0.1 release-prep: document /spawn --abandon {team} flow in commands/spawn.md. Repo: agent-teams. File boundaries: commands/spawn.md. Lint: echo docs-no-lint. Patterns to follow: commands/spawn.md. Notes from explorer: See Arm D of the refined plan. ADD only — do not modify existing sections of commands/spawn.md. Document /spawn --abandon [team] [--force] flow: discover open beads issues with team tag, worktrees team-*, branches agent/{team}/* and integration/{team}; show preview; ask confirmation unless --force; on confirmation: bd close each with reason 'abandoned by /spawn --abandon', git worktree remove --force, git branch -D. Two safety rules: (1) NEVER delete branches pushed to remote (git branch -r check); (2) NEVER delete if integration/{team} has unmerged commits (git log main..integration/{team} check). Per orchestrator decision: ABORT the entire abandon operation on FIRST safety trip, print the tripped rule + resolution, exit non-zero without touching anything. Docs-only; no executable test; unambiguous enough for orchestrator to execute from docs alone.. Model: sonnet. If model is a gpt-* id, agent delegates to `codex exec` in the worktree; otherwise implements directly with Claude tools. Commits with HUSKY=0 (or repo equivalent). Does not push.

title: mol-implement-arm
description: Child formula: one implementer working on one slice of a larger feature. The explorer bonds one of these per arm it identifies. Each arm owns a non-overlapping set of files and commits in its own worktree. Not meant to be invoked directly — use full-team or lite-team.

title: [selfimp] implement-docs: v0.1 release-prep: CHANGELOG, CONTRIBUTING, move Troubleshooting out of README
description: Implement docs portion of v0.1 release-prep: CHANGELOG, CONTRIBUTING, move Troubleshooting out of README. Repo: agent-teams. File boundaries: CHANGELOG.md,CONTRIBUTING.md,docs/troubleshooting.md,README.md. Lint: echo docs-no-lint. Patterns to follow: README.md,docs/architecture.md. Notes from explorer: See Arm B of the refined plan. CHANGELOG follows Keep a Changelog format, populates [0.1.0] - 2026-04-18 from git log main (~15-19 entries grouped Added/Changed/Fixed). CONTRIBUTING covers: adding stack profiles, formulas, agents, testing before PR, commit conventions. Move the 6-entry Troubleshooting section from README into docs/troubleshooting.md verbatim; replace in README with a one-line pointer. Add a --local install issues stub in docs/troubleshooting.md. README edits are LIMITED to the Troubleshooting section and a pointer to docs/troubleshooting.md — do not touch other README sections (other arms aren't editing README, this arm owns the file).. Model: sonnet. If model is a gpt-* id, agent delegates to `codex exec` in the worktree; otherwise implements directly with Claude tools. Commits with HUSKY=0 (or repo equivalent). Does not push.

title: [selfimp] implement-templates: v0.1 release-prep: example plan templates for /spawn discovery
description: Implement templates portion of v0.1 release-prep: example plan templates for /spawn discovery. Repo: agent-teams. File boundaries: examples/plan-templates/TEMPLATE.md,examples/plan-templates/multi-milestone-TEMPLATE.md,examples/plan-templates/README.md. Lint: echo docs-no-lint. Patterns to follow: docs/writing-plans.md. Notes from explorer: See Arm C of the refined plan. Two templates: (1) TEMPLATE.md = generic single-milestone, matches /spawn built-in scaffold (sections: problem statement, out of scope, milestones [checkboxes], testing, progress log). (2) multi-milestone-TEMPLATE.md = for mol-plan-driven-team, with nested milestone sub-checkboxes, Arms section describing parallelism, Stack notes placeholder. Both use {{name}}, {{feature}}, {{date}} placeholders. README.md explains how /spawn discovers templates, placeholder substitution rules, when to use which, how to drop into a consuming repo's docs/plans/TEMPLATE.md to override the built-in scaffold.. Model: sonnet. If model is a gpt-* id, agent delegates to `codex exec` in the worktree; otherwise implements directly with Claude tools. Commits with HUSKY=0 (or repo equivalent). Does not push.

File boundaries: install.sh,uninstall.sh,.github/workflows/validate.yml,scripts/validate-formulas.py. Lint: bash -n. Patterns to follow: install.sh,uninstall.sh. Notes from explorer: See /Users/sean.ransom/.claude/plans/agent-teams-self-improvements-v0.1.refined.md Arm A section plus the 'Orchestrator decisions' appendix. Key constraints: (1) validate-formulas.py has HARD CAP 150 lines Python with EXACTLY 4 checks (JSON+required-keys, mol-* naming+filename match, needs+waits_for:all-children trap, var-reference integrity); (2) --dry-run and --local compose orthogonally; (3) uninstall aborts on first safety trip; (4) fix 'Install 5 formulas' → '6' banner in install.sh.. Model: sonnet. If model is a gpt-* id, agent delegates to `codex exec` in the worktree; otherwise implements directly with Claude tools. Commits with HUSKY=0 (or repo equivalent). Does not push.

title: [selfimp] explore + decompose: v0.1 release-prep: install/CI, docs, templates, abandon-path
description: Map the codebase for v0.1 release-prep: install/CI, docs, templates, abandon-path using the verified plan at /Users/sean.ransom/.claude/plans/agent-teams-self-improvements-v0.1.md (or /Users/sean.ransom/.claude/plans/agent-teams-self-improvements-v0.1.md.refined.md if plan-checker produced one and use_refined_plan is true). Decompose into implementer arms with non-overlapping file boundaries. Bond one mol-implement-arm per arm. Set cross-arm dependencies in beads. Report decomposition. Model: haiku.
close_reason: Explore done. 4 arms bonded + 20 gating deps wired.

title: [selfimp] code-review: integration/selfimp branch
description: Run code review on integration/selfimp branch. Report P1/P2 findings. Model: sonnet.

title: [selfimp] codex-review: correctness + spec-adherence pass on integration/selfimp
description: Run Codex CLI against the changed files for mechanical correctness (wrong fields, missing null checks, off-by-one). Report P1/P2. Do NOT edit files. Skip gracefully if codex is not installed.

title: mol-plan-driven-team
description: Same as mol-full-team with a plan-check step prepended and a plan-update step appended. Plan-checker verifies the plan is concrete enough to execute and writes a refined sidecar if needed. At integration, the orchestrator ticks checkboxes in the plan file for work that got done. Use when your plan is long, multi-milestone, or still rough.

title: [selfimp] integrate: merge arms, merge to base branch, tick plan, clean up
description: 1. Create integration/selfimp, merge all arm + test-writer branches into it. 2. Fix P1 findings from both reviewers. 3. Update the plan: for every milestone in /Users/sean.ransom/.claude/plans/agent-teams-self-improvements-v0.1.md (or the refined version) that got shipped by this team, tick its - [ ] checkbox to - [x]. Append a '## Progress log' entry with team name, date, arms, and linked commits. 4. Push integration/selfimp to origin. 5. Merge into base branch, push. 6. Delete branches + worktrees. 7. Close remaining open beads issues.

title: [selfimp] test-writer: write tests for v0.1 release-prep: install/CI, docs, templates, abandon-path
description: Write colocated tests for all new source files across all arms. Run the repo's test command and confirm tests pass. Model: haiku.

title: [selfimp] judge: verify all implementations
description: Judge all implementer arms (attempt 1 of 3). VERDICT: PASS unblocks test + review. VERDICT: FAIL → reopen failed arm(s), retry. Max 3 attempts. If a FAIL is rooted in plan ambiguity (not implementer error), flag it so the orchestrator can re-run plan-check. Model: sonnet.

title: [selfimp] plan-check: verify /Users/sean.ransom/.claude/plans/agent-teams-self-improvements-v0.1.md is ready to execute
description: Read the plan at /Users/sean.ransom/.claude/plans/agent-teams-self-improvements-v0.1.md. Check for concrete problem statement, verifiable milestones (prefer - [ ] checkboxes), specific file paths and signatures, missing pieces (error handling, tests, auth), and stack fit. Produce PLAN_VERDICT: PASS / REFINED / NEEDS_REVISION. On REFINED, write /Users/sean.ransom/.claude/plans/agent-teams-self-improvements-v0.1.md.refined.md. On NEEDS_REVISION, stop the run and return to user. SHORTCUT: if /Users/sean.ransom/.claude/plans/agent-teams-self-improvements-v0.1.md ends in .refined.md, return PLAN_VERDICT: PASS immediately without re-checking — it's the output of a prior plan-check and re-refining is wasted work.
close_reason: PLAN_VERDICT: REFINED with 4 orchestrator decisions appended — using refined plan for downstream
